title: FTC v. Wyndham Worldwide Corp. (2015) — Section 5 Cybersecurity and Third Circuit Affirmance
forum: U.S. District Court (D.N.J.) / Third Circuit / FTC

timeline.event: FTC files federal complaint (payment card breaches and security practices)
District court denies motion to dismiss in relevant part

evidence_artifacts: Network segmentation diagrams and rule reviews for property-to-corporate paths
Remote access and privileged account authentication evidence
Logging and monitoring coverage for cardholder-data environments
PCI DSS assessment reports and remediation tracking (per order)
Incident response records across repeated intrusions (historical)
engineering_takeaways: Treat franchise and property connectivity as a regulated trust boundary.
Repeated breaches without durable control uplift increase regulatory and litigation risk.
Public security claims must match implemented controls and monitoring.
